english: to evade, to shun, to go out of the way, jur-méd ('jur med) unavoidable Mil.
Jébs-po ('jebs po), well-sounding Stg.; snyan - °jébs (snyan 'jebs) harmony, euphony.
1. dexterity, cleverness Lex. 2. skilled, clever; Sch. decent; jéms-po ('jems po) id.
a coquettish, alluring, seducing attitude or posture; Lex.: °jud-mtún °jo-sgég °jog ('jud mthun 'jo sgeg 'jog) the harlot assumes such an attitude.

pf. bżos (bzhos), ft. bżo (bzho), imp. °jos ('jos), to milk, rá-ma °jó-ba (ra ma 'jo ba) to milk a goat, °o-ma °jó-ba ('o ma 'jo ba) 'to milk the milk'; kyód-kyis °o-ma bżos dug (khyod kyis 'o ma bzhos dug), ṅas ni bżos-pa med (ngas ni bzhos pa med), it is you, not I, that have 'milked out the milk' Glr.; °jó(-ba)-po ('jo ba po), °jó-mkan ('jo mkhan), milker, milk-man, °jó(-ba)-mo ('jo ba mo) milkmaid; °dód-°joi ba ('dod 'jo'i ba) a cow that is able to fulfil every wish.

I. pf. bżag (bzhag), ft. gżag (gzhag), imp. żog (zhog), C. col. *żág-pa* (*zhag pa*), 1. to put, to place, e.g. the foot on the ground; also to place persons, to assign them a place Dzl., Glr.; fig. = °gód-pa ('god pa) (e.g. dgé-ba-la (dge ba la), byaṅ-cúb-la (byang chub la), byaṅ-cúb-kyi lám-la (byang chub kyi lam la)) v. gód-pa ('god pa) 3; to put in order, to arrange, °jig-rtén-bżag-pa ('jig rten bzhag pa) the arrangement (system) of the world; lus dráṅ-por bżag-ste (lus drang por bzhag ste) sitting straight, bolt-upright Dzl., Mil.; bżág-na mi sdod (bzhag na mi sdod) if one places her any where, she will not remain there Mil.; stéṅ-du yar bżag (steng du yar bzhag) (the anchors) were placed above, were weighed Pth.; lás-su °jóg-pa (las su 'jog pa) to set one a task, to employ one in a certain service Dzl., rgyal-sríd-la °jóg-pa (rgyal srid la 'jog pa) to appoint one to the government i.e. to make one king; séms (sems) (resp. tugs (thugs))-la °jóg-pa ('jog pa) to take to heart Glr., Mil.; lús-la grui °du-sés bżág-la (lus la gru'i 'du shes bzhag la) if we fancy the human body to be a ship Thgy.; nám-mk̃a ráṅ-gi ṅáṅ-du żog (nam mkha' rang gi ngang du zhog) transfer it to the nature of the ethereal space, i.e. figure it to yourself as ether Mil.; pýir °jóg-pa (phyir 'jog pa) 1. to leave behind, at home Dzl.; 2. to put by, to lay aside Dzl.; (another reading omits pýir (phyir)). — 2. to lay or put down, a burden etc., *żóg-la dog* (*zhog la dog*) put (it) down and come! C.; nor (g)sog °jog med (nor gsog 'jog med) heaping up treasures and depositing them was not, i.e. was never heard of; gsóg-°jog-mkan (gsog 'jog mkhan) a hoarder up, a miser Cs.; to leave, to leave behind, lag-rjés (lag rjes) a trace or mark of activity, monumentum Glr.; to leave, quit, abandon, ráṅ-gi yul (rang gi yul) one's own country Glr.; póṅs-par ma bżág-par (phongs par ma bzhag par) so that it is not abandoned, given up, to poverty Thgy.; *yúg-le żog* (*yug le zhog*) C. (= *páṅ-te bor* (*phang te bor*) W.) throw it away! to depose, yí-ger bris °jóg-pa (yi ger bris 'jog pa) to depose in writing, literis mandare Glr.; sá-boṅ (sa bon), gduṅ-brgyúd °jóg-pa (gdung brgyud 'jog pa) to leave an offspring behind, to propagate the species; to lay up, to keep, as holy relics; to lay aside, ré-żig żóg-la (re zhig zhog la) setting aside, apart, for a while Dzl.; mnyám-par °jóg-pa (mnyam par 'jog pa) v. mnyám-pa (mnyam pa); sgról-lam °jog (sgrol lam 'jog) shall we turn them out or leave them? Mil. nt. II. pf. (b)żògs ((b)zhogs), fut. gżog (gzhog), imp. żog (zhog), W. *żog-ċe* (*zhog ce*), to cut, to hew, to square, a pen, timber etc.; to carve, to chip, a thin piece of wood etc.
n. of a Lu Mil., = jag-po (jag po).

col., Sch. °jóṅ-po ('jong po), oblong, longish, oval, elliptical, cylindric, bottle-shaped etc.; col. also applied to stature: tall; °joṅ-nyáms-ċan ('jong nyams can) Wdṅ. oblong shaped, in relation to leaves, cones of fir etc.; ló-ma °joṅ-stábs nyág-ga-ċan (lo ma 'jong stabs nyag ga can) split into narrow slips, wing-cleft (leaves of caraway) Wdṅ.; dbyibs-°jóṅ (dbyibs 'jong) an oval form.

pf. bċom (bcom), also żom (zhom), fut. gżom (gzhom), imp. ċom (com), W. *ċóm-ċe* (*com ce*) 1. to conquer, subdue, oppress, suppress, an enemy; °dód-ċágs-kyis kún-nas °jóms-pa ('dod chags kyis kun nas 'joms pa) to be quite overpowered by lust; nad °jóms-pai sman (nad 'joms pa'i sman) a medicine for a disease (to overcome it); ráb-tu gżóm-pa (rab tu gzhom pa) °di ('di) the following overpowering (charm);
